title_guess: Flecknoe (Bush Hill Farm), Oxford Canal villages

pages.text:
sun. Simon was quite red, too, John hardly pink at all. It was very windy.
        __Wednesday May 30th__
        We set off for home at 9.50, Eve driving, because Simon's glasses were broken, & arrive at noon.

        Friday June 22nd.
TEC  We set off just after 6 for __Flecknoe__. It's a village east of Leamington Spa. It's on an amazing flat hill: fantastic views from our field on 3 sides of the camping van, including the radio masts at Daventry & a farm at the end of the field. Full of sheep. Charles has a tent. William has just left for Guatemala. ***
__Bush Hill Farm__
        Saturday.
        Cool to begin with. Warm by midday, but with a nice breeze. We set off on a bike ride along the canal tow path which was not in good repair. So after that we went along roads. We went through Lower Shuckborough, [?Around] Grandborough, Sawbridge, Wolfhamcote, & back to Flecknoe. Lower Shuckborough has

a fine church with double dog-tooth arches + wooden roof. A woman + children came to do the flowers, so we were let in. At Grandborough we had a good lunch at "the Shoulder of Mutton". Simon had home-baked ham, Eve had scampi Charles had Riche. There was a pond.
      Wolfhamcote is deserted. The canal had an extra spur there. The fine stone church is closed up.
      We cycled about 10 miles. I need a sheepskin saddle. Tonic rode in my bike bag like ET, but also ran on the lead about 8 miles.
      We all fell asleep when we got back + then had a cup of tea.
      For the rest of the day we read + wrote. Simon + Charles went on a short bike ride round the village, + did maths. We are surrounded by stupid, messy sheep
      Fantastic views as we went to bed + a new moon.

[Glued-in pictorial map postcard of The Oxford Canal]

      Sunday blue sky, hot sunshine when we woke up. By 9.30. we were at Wolfhamcote church, where we left the van + cycled off. The church Key is kept in a box at the manor house, so we were able to see inside. It's very beautiful.
